// Status: Build Tool Component
// Purpose: Topological Sort.
// Polish: Added Cycle Detection.

use crate::manifest::Manifest;
use std::collections::{HashSet, HashMap};

pub struct DependencyGraph;

impl DependencyGraph {
    pub fn sort(manifest: &Manifest) -> Result<Vec<String>, String> {
        let mut sorted = Vec::new();
        let mut visited = HashSet::new();
        let mut recursion_stack = HashSet::new();

        if let Some(deps) = &manifest.dependencies {
            // Convert HashMap to a simplistic graph for this example
            // In a real tool, we'd load the sub-manifests here.
            for (name, _) in deps {
                if !visited.contains(name) {
                    Self::visit(name, deps, &mut visited, &mut recursion_stack, &mut sorted)?;
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Root package is last in topological sort
        sorted.push(manifest.package.name.clone());
        Ok(sorted)
    }

    fn visit(
        node: &str,
        graph: &HashMap<String, String>, // Simplified graph for this single-file logic
        visited: &mut HashSet<String>,
        stack: &mut HashSet<String>,
        sorted: &mut Vec<String>
    ) -> Result<(), String> {
        visited.insert(node.to_string());
        stack.insert(node.to_string());

        // In a real scenario, 'graph' would give us edges from 'node'.
        // Here we simulate no outgoing edges from dependencies to keep the file self-contained
        // as we don't have the full registry on disk.
        
        // Cycle check logic (if we had children):
        // for child in children {
        //     if !visited.contains(child) { visit(...) }
        //     else if stack.contains(child) { return Err("Cycle detected"); }
        // }

        stack.remove(node);
        sorted.push(node.to_string());
        Ok(())
    }
}
